title: Remote Desktop and Screen-Sharing Setup
text:
Q: How do I set up remote desktop and screen-sharing on my computer?
A: Windows: Step 1: Open the Start menu and type 'Remote Desktop Settings', then select it. Step 2: Toggle 'Enable Remote Desktop' to On. Step 3: Note the name of your PC under 'How to connect to this PC'. Step 4: Ensure your firewall allows Remote Desktop connections. macOS: Step 1: Open 'System Preferences' and select 'Sharing'. Step 2: Check the box next to 'Screen Sharing' or 'Remote Management' depending on your needs. Step 3: Note the IP address shown under 'Screen Sharing: On'. Step 4: Click 'Computer Settings' to set a password for VNC viewers if needed. Windows: Step 5: To connect from another Windows PC, open 'Remote Desktop Connection' from the Start menu, enter the PC name, and click 'Connect'. macOS: Step 5: To connect from another Mac, open 'Finder', select 'Go' > 'Connect to Server', enter 'vnc://[IP address]', and click 'Connect'. Step 6: Enter your credentials when prompted. Step 7: For both OS, ensure you have a stable internet connection for optimal performance.
